news.title: West Bengal government sends three-page SOP form to builders for construction audit
news.para: Kolkata's state government has issued a new Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) requiring builders to submit detailed project information to borough offices, a move welcomed by major developers for its decentralised approach. This initiative aims to prevent future incidents like the Taratala warehouse collapse. Developers are cooperating fully, ensuring key personnel are available for site inspections to expedite project clearances and resume construction work promptly.

news.title: Maharashtra infuses another ₹30 crore, takes metro phase-II equity to ₹607 crore
news.para: Maharashtra government has released an additional ₹30 crore for the Nagpur Metro Phase-2, bringing its total equity contribution to ₹607 crore. This funding supports the 43.8-km project, which recently saw a proposed 1.4-km extension of the Kamptee corridor approved. Land acquisition is progressing well, with over 50% secured, and critical construction milestones are being met, indicating steady momentum for the ambitious metro expansion.

news.title: Building homes around customers: VTP Realty's ₹14,000-crore launch portfolio
news.para: Delivering 8,000+ homes and over 10 million sq. ft. this fiscal, and the company's outlook on future opportunities in the Mumbai market.

news.title: Protest erupts in LDA colony over proposed Lucknow civic body's 60-shop vending zone
news.para: Residents of LDA Colony protested a proposed vending zone in Aashiana, claiming it's a permanent structure violating planning norms.  Locals allege the Lucknow Municipal Corporation is building over 60 shops, despite official claims of a temporary setup.  Concerns are raised about lost footpath space, increased congestion near hospitals and schools, and potential legal action if construction isn't halted.

news.title: Ghaziabad: After fire safety push in condos, RWAs flag high costs, lack of residents’ cooperation, old infra
news.para: Resident welfare associations in Ghaziabad are struggling to meet stringent fire safety directives, citing prohibitive costs for maintaining equipment, outdated electrical systems, and difficulties in keeping emergency exits clear.  Developers often hand over projects before full compliance, leaving associations with hefty upgrade bills and resident reluctance to pay.  Governance gaps and a lack of consistent enforcement further complicate efforts to ensure safety.

news.title: Omaxe plans ₹6,200 crore investment to develop 19 hotels
news.para: Omaxe plans to develop 19 hotels across 13 cities in five states over the next four-five years. The proposed hospitality portfolio will cover nearly five million sq ft and will be developed across urban centres, pilgrimage destinations and transit corridors.

news.title: UK's Bridgepoint to buy Kayne Anderson Real Estate in $1.39 billion deal
news.para: London-based Bridgepoint is acquiring U.S. firm Kayne Anderson's real estate arm for approximately $1.4 billion. This strategic move aims to diversify income and boost international reach, adding $22 billion in property assets to Bridgepoint's existing $117 billion portfolio. The deal, comprising cash and new shares, is expected to significantly enhance Bridgepoint's earnings and solidify its global presence in private markets.

news.title: Ahead of monsoon, MCD surveys 28 lakh buildings in Delhi, flags just 19 as 'dangerous'
news.para: Delhi's recent pre-monsoon survey, which examined around 28 lakh buildings, identified a scant 19 structures deemed hazardous, igniting concerns about its validity. Despite the Municipal Corporation of Delhi asserting an 85% completion rate, experts denounce the purely observational approach. Moreover, the unfortunate reality of building collapses occurring in reportedly safe buildings emphasizes the glaring inadequacies of this yearly review process.

news.title: Credai Karnataka pushes for quicker approvals to cut red tape
news.para: Developers in Karnataka are facing significant project delays and home delivery issues due to prolonged approval processes and procedural bottlenecks, despite government efforts to streamline sanctions. Builders highlight issues like e-khata delays, contradictory rules, and lengthy NOCs, impacting cash flow and investor confidence. Industry leaders are urging for integrated single-window clearance mechanisms and fixed timelines to boost efficiency and support urban growth.

news.title: Puravankara to jointly develop 6.4-acre land parcel in Bengaluru
news.para: The land parcel in Sarjapur has a saleable area of about 0.8 million sq ft and an estimated gross development value of ₹1,000 crore. With this transaction, Puravankara’s total potential GDV from acquisitions and JDAs in Q1 FY27 stands at ₹5,200 crore.

news.title: Audit to start today; West Bengal government asks builders to take care of workers
news.para: Kolkata's construction sector braces for a comprehensive safety audit of under-construction high-rises starting Monday across multiple districts. Developers are urged to support workers during the temporary work suspension, with Credai pledging food, shelter, and financial aid. This initiative follows concerns over worker welfare and aims to prevent future construction mishaps, ensuring adherence to safety standards and building codes.

news.title: Panchkula civic body set to take over 11 HSVP sectors
news.para: Haryana government is set to consolidate civic governance in Panchkula, transferring 11 sectors from HSVP to the municipal corporation. This move aims to end jurisdictional confusion and establish clear accountability for residents. Mayor Sham Lal Bansal announced the plan, which will bring all public departments under the MC's umbrella, promising faster grievance resolution and streamlined development across the city's wards.

news.title: Ahead of June 30 rebate deadline, glitches mar Delhi civic body's property tax portal
news.para: Delhi residents are facing technical issues on the MCD portal just days before the property tax rebate deadline. Mandatory electricity consumer number entry and a slow portal are causing frustration. Residents also report confusion over user charges and the integration of trade license systems, with some feeling unfairly taxed on commercial portions of their properties.

news.title: Incuspaze raises ₹150 crore to fuel expansion ahead of FY29 IPO
news.para: Flexible workspace provider Incuspaze has secured Rs 150 crore in a significant funding round led by Bharat Value Fund. This capital injection will fuel expansion in key markets, boost technology investments, and support acquisitions as the company eyes a public listing in FY29. The funding follows Incuspaze's recent acquisition of iKeva, bolstering its presence in Hyderabad and its overall growth strategy.

news.title: Hyderabad homebuyers left in limbo as over 60,000 flats miss delivery timeline
news.para: Thousands of Hyderabad homebuyers face significant delays, with nearly 63,000 units now expected to be handed over at least a year later than planned.  Disruptions from the Middle East conflict, impacting material imports and escalating costs, coupled with labour shortages, are pushing project completion dates back. Many buyers are burdened by dual EMI and rental payments, awaiting their dream homes.

news.title: Salman Khan family’s sea-facing Bandra residential project gets MCZMA’s clearance
news.para: Salman Khan's mother, Salma Khan, has received a crucial clearance from the Maharashtra Coastal Zone Management Authority for a new sea-facing residential building in Bandra. The project, a ground-plus-six-storey structure, will be built on a plot previously owned by Mary Clotilda Baptista. The MCZMA emphasized adherence to existing land use and FSI norms from January 18, 2019, for the redevelopment.

news.title: Prestige Group holds ₹65,000 crore unrecognised revenue in books on record pre-sales: Chairman
news.para: Prestige Estates Projects holds a substantial Rs 65,000 crore in unrecognised revenue, fueled by robust housing sales. The company achieved record bookings of Rs 30,024 crore in FY26 and anticipates even stronger performance this fiscal year, targeting Rs 35,000-36,000 crore in sales.  Discussions are underway to adopt a more immediate revenue recognition method.

news.title: Chandigarh allows bed & breakfasts in residential areas
news.para: Chandigarh's residential sectors are set for a transformation as the administration permits Bed & Breakfast (BnB) operations in large homes.  Eligible houses, at least 500 square yards, can now host tourists under a new tourism department policy. This move, however, faces strong opposition from residents concerned about commercialization, increased traffic, and noise.  Officials aim to boost tourism and homeowner income, learning from a previous failed 2008 scheme.

news.title: Embassy Developments to invest ₹1,500 crore to build three million sq ft office space in Bengaluru
news.para: Embassy Developments is investing Rs 1,500 crore to build a 3 million sq ft office complex in Bengaluru, aiming to boost rental income from commercial assets. This move complements their strong residential business, which saw sales bookings more than double last fiscal. The company plans to launch nearly Rs 20,000 crore worth of homes this fiscal, targeting robust consumer demand.

news.title: Prime Hyderabad housing societies’ land caught in decades-old legal maze
news.para: Hyderabad's prime areas like Banjara Hills and Jubilee Hills are embroiled in decades-old land disputes, with thousands of acres under litigation. Overlapping claims, fake survey numbers, and outdated records fuel legal battles between housing societies, private owners, and the state government.  Revenue authorities acknowledge discrepancies, leaving residents in limbo and impacting property transactions.
